california_schools||1.question: Please list the lowest three eligible free rates for students aged 5-17 in continuation schools.
california_schools||1.evidence: Eligible free rates for students aged 5-17 = `Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17)` / `Enrollment (Ages 5-17)`
california_schools||1.gold: SELECT `Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17)` / `Enrollment (Ages 5-17)` FROM frpm WHERE `Educational Option Type` = 'Continuation School' AND `Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17)` / `Enrollment (Ages 5-17)` IS NOT NULL ORDER BY `Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17)` / `Enrollment (Ages 5-17)` ASC LIMIT 3
samples: SELECT School Name, (Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17) / Enrollment (Ages 5-17)) AS Eligible_Free_Rate FROM frpm WHERE School Type = 'Continuation' ORDER BY Eligible_Free_Rate ASC LIMIT 3;
SELECT School Name, (Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17) / Enrollment (Ages 5-17)) AS Eligible_Free_Rate FROM frpm WHERE School Type = 'Continuation' ORDER BY Eligible_Free_Rate ASC LIMIT 3;
SELECT School Name, (Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17) / Enrollment (Ages 5-17)) AS Eligible_Free_Rate FROM frpm WHERE School Type = 'Continuation' ORDER BY Eligible_Free_Rate ASC LIMIT 3;

SELECT s.School Name, (f.Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17) / f.Enrollment (Ages 5-17)) AS Eligible_Free_Rate FROM frpm f JOIN schools s ON f.CDSCode = s.CDSCode WHERE s.School Type = 'Continuation' ORDER BY Eligible_Free_Rate ASC LIMIT 3;
SELECT School Name, (Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17) / Enrollment (Ages 5-17)) AS Eligible_Free_Rate FROM frpm WHERE School Type = 'Continuation School' ORDER BY Eligible_Free_Rate ASC LIMIT 3;

SELECT s.CDSCode, s.SchoolName, (f."Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17)" / f."Enrollment (Ages 5-17)") AS EligibleFreeRate FROM frpm f JOIN schools s ON f.CDSCode = s.CDSCode WHERE s.SchoolType = 'Continuation' ORDER BY EligibleFreeRate ASC LIMIT 3;

SELECT sname, dname, cname, (`Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17)` / `Enrollment (Ages 5-17)`) AS Eligible_Free_Rate FROM frpm JOIN schools ON frpm.CDSCode = schools.CDSCode WHERE School Type = 'Continuation' ORDER BY Eligible_Free_Rate ASC LIMIT 3;

SELECT s.CDSCode, s.School Name, (f.Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17) / f.Enrollment (Ages 5-17)) AS EligibleFreeRate FROM frpm f JOIN schools s ON f.CDSCode = s.CDSCode WHERE s.School Type = 'Continuation' ORDER BY EligibleFreeRate ASC LIMIT 3;

california_schools||12.question: Among the schools with an SAT excellence rate of over 0.3, what is the highest eligible free rate for students aged 5-17?
california_schools||12.evidence: Excellence rate = NumGE1500 / NumTstTakr; Eligible free rates for students aged 5-17 = `Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17)` / `Enrollment (Ages 5-17)`
california_schools||12.gold: SELECT MAX(CAST(T1.`Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17)` AS REAL) / T1.`Enrollment (Ages 5-17)`) FROM frpm AS T1 INNER JOIN satscores AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.cds WHERE CAST(T2.NumGE1500 AS REAL) / T2.NumTstTakr > 0.3
samples: SELECT MAX((FreeMealCountAges5To17 / EnrollmentAges5To17)) AS HighestEligibleFreeRate FROM (SELECT f.`Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17)` AS FreeMealCountAges5To17, f.`Enrollment (Ages 5-17)` AS EnrollmentAges5To17, s.NumGE1500 / s.NumTstTakr AS ExcellenceRate FROM frpm f JOIN satscores s ON f.CDSCode = s.cds WHERE s.NumTstTakr > 0 AND (s.NumGE1500 / s.NumTstTakr) > 0.3);
SELECT MAX((Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17) / Enrollment (Ages 5-17)) * 100) FROM frpm JOIN satscores ON frpm.CDSCode = satscores.cds WHERE (NumGE1500 / NumTstTakr) > 0.3;

SELECT MAX((Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17) / Enrollment (Ages 5-17)) * 100) AS HighestEligibleFreeRate FROM frpm JOIN satscores ON frpm.CDSCode = satscores.cds WHERE (NumGE1500 * 1.0 / NumTstTakr) > 0.3;

SELECT MAX((FreeMealCountAges5To17 / EnrollmentAges5To17)) AS HighestEligibleFreeRate FROM (SELECT (NumGE1500 * 1.0 / NumTstTakr) AS ExcellenceRate, frpm."Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17)" AS FreeMealCountAges5To17, frpm."Enrollment (Ages 5-17)" AS EnrollmentAges5To17 FROM satscores JOIN frpm ON satscores.cds = frpm.CDSCode WHERE (NumGE1500 * 1.0 / NumTstTakr) > 0.3);

california_schools||41.question: List the names of virtual schools that are among the top 5 in their respective counties based on average reading scores.
california_schools||41.evidence: Exclusively virtual refers to Virtual = 'F'; respective counties means PARTITION BY County
california_schools||41.gold: SELECT School FROM (SELECT T2.School,T1.AvgScrRead, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY T2.County ORDER BY T1.AvgScrRead DESC) AS rnk FROM satscores AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.cds = T2.CDSCode WHERE T2.Virtual = 'F' ) ranked_schools WHERE rnk <= 5
samples: SELECT s.School AS SchoolName FROM satscores sat JOIN schools s ON sat.cds = s.CDSCode WHERE s.Virtual = 'F' AND sat.rtype = 'reading' ORDER BY sat.AvgScrRead DESC LIMIT 5;

SELECT s.School AS SchoolName, s.County AS CountyName FROM schools s JOIN satscores sa ON s.CDSCode = sa.cds WHERE s.Virtual = 'F' AND sa.rtype = 'Reading' ORDER BY sa.AvgScrRead DESC LIMIT 5;

SELECT sname FROM satscores JOIN schools ON satscores.cds = schools.CDSCode WHERE schools.Virtual = 'F' AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM satscores s2 JOIN schools s3 ON s2.cds = s3.CDSCode WHERE s3.Virtual = 'F' AND s3.County = schools.County AND s2.AvgScrRead > satscores.AvgScrRead) < 5;

SELECT sname FROM satscores JOIN schools ON satscores.cds = schools.CDSCode WHERE schools.Virtual = 'F' AND (sname, AvgScrRead) IN (SELECT sname, AvgScrRead FROM satscores JOIN schools ON satscores.cds = schools.CDSCode WHERE schools.Virtual = 'F' ORDER BY AvgScrRead DESC LIMIT 5) ORDER BY County;

SELECT sname FROM satscores INNER JOIN schools ON satscores.cds = schools.CDSCode WHERE schools.Virtual = 'F' AND sname IN (SELECT sname FROM satscores WHERE rtype = 'Reading' ORDER BY AvgScrRead DESC LIMIT 5) ORDER BY County;

financial||94.question: List out the account numbers of female clients who are oldest and has lowest average salary, calculate the gap between this lowest average salary with the highest average salary?

financial||185.question: What proportion of customers who have accounts at the Prague branch are female?
financial||185.evidence: Female refers to gender = 'F'; Percentage of female clients in Prague branch = count[female clients with accounts in Prague branch / count(clients with accounts in Prague branch)] * 100%; A3 may contain information about Prague
financial||185.gold: SELECT CAST(SUM(T2.gender = 'F') AS REAL) / COUNT(T2.client_id) * 100 FROM district AS T1 INNER JOIN client AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id WHERE T1.A3 = 'Prague'
samples: SELECT (COUNT(DISTINCT c.client_id) * 100.0 / (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT c1.client_id) FROM client c1 JOIN disp d ON c1.client_id = d.client_id JOIN account a ON d.account_id = a.account_id WHERE a.district_id = (SELECT district_id FROM district WHERE A3 = 'Prague'))) AS female_percentage FROM client c JOIN disp d ON c.client_id = d.client_id JOIN account a ON d.account_id = a.account_id WHERE a.district_id = (SELECT district_id FROM district WHERE A3 = 'Prague') AND c.gender = 'F';

SELECT (COUNT(CASE WHEN c.gender = 'F' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*)) AS female_percentage FROM client c JOIN account a ON c.district_id = a.district_id WHERE a.district_id IN (SELECT district_id FROM district WHERE A3 = 'Prague');

financial||186.question: What percentage of male clients request for weekly statements to be issued?

formula_1||954.question: Please calculate the race completion percentage of Japanese drivers from 2007 to 2009.
formula_1||954.evidence: from 2007 to 2009 refers to year between 2007 and 2009; race completion refers to time is not null; percentage = Divide(COUNT(DriverID where time is not null and year between 2007 and 2009),Count (DriverID where year between 2007 and 2009))*100;
formula_1||954.gold: SELECT CAST(SUM(IIF(T1.time IS NOT NULL, 1, 0)) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T1.raceId) FROM results AS T1 INNER JOIN races AS T2 on T1.raceId = T2.raceId INNER JOIN drivers AS T3 on T1.driverId = T3.driverId WHERE T3.nationality = 'Japanese' AND T2.year BETWEEN 2007 AND 2009
samples: SELECT (COUNT(DISTINCT l.driverId) * 100.0 / COUNT(DISTINCT d.driverId)) AS completion_percentage FROM lapTimes l JOIN results r ON l.raceId = r.raceId JOIN drivers d ON r.driverId = d.driverId WHERE l.time IS NOT NULL AND d.nationality = 'Japanese' AND r.raceId IN (SELECT raceId FROM races WHERE year BETWEEN 2007 AND 2009);

SELECT (COUNT(DISTINCT l.driverId) * 100.0 / COUNT(DISTINCT d.driverId)) AS completion_percentage FROM lapTimes l JOIN drivers d ON l.driverId = d.driverId JOIN results r ON l.raceId = r.raceId JOIN races ra ON r.raceId = ra.raceId WHERE d.nationality = 'Japanese' AND ra.year BETWEEN 2007 AND 2009 AND l.time IS NOT NULL;

SELECT (COUNT(DISTINCT results.driverId) * 100.0 / (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT driverId) FROM results JOIN drivers ON results.driverId = drivers.driverId WHERE drivers.nationality = 'Japanese' AND results.raceId IN (SELECT raceId FROM races WHERE year BETWEEN 2007 AND 2009))) AS completion_percentage FROM results JOIN drivers ON results.driverId = drivers.driverId WHERE drivers.nationality = 'Japanese' AND results.time IS NOT NULL AND results.raceId IN (SELECT raceId FROM races WHERE year BETWEEN 2007 AND 2009);

SELECT (COUNT(DISTINCT results.driverId) * 100.0 / (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT results.driverId) FROM results JOIN drivers ON results.driverId = drivers.driverId WHERE drivers.nationality = 'Japanese' AND results.raceId IN (SELECT raceId FROM races WHERE year BETWEEN 2007 AND 2009))) AS completion_percentage FROM results JOIN drivers ON results.driverId = drivers.driverId WHERE drivers.nationality = 'Japanese' AND results.time IS NOT NULL AND results.raceId IN (SELECT raceId FROM races WHERE year BETWEEN 2007 AND 2009);

formula_1||955.question: What is the average time in seconds of champion for each year, before year 1975?
formula_1||955.evidence: only champion's finished time is represented by 'HH:MM:SS.mmm'; finished the game refers to time is not null; before year 1975 refers to year < 1975;
formula_1||955.gold: WITH time_in_seconds AS ( SELECT T2.year, T2.raceId, T1.positionOrder, CASE WHEN T1.positionOrder = 1 THEN (CAST(SUBSTR(T1.time, 1, 1) AS REAL) * 3600) + (CAST(SUBSTR(T1.time, 3, 2) AS REAL) * 60) + CAST(SUBSTR(T1.time, 6,2) AS REAL )   + CAST(SUBSTR(T1.time, 9) AS REAL)/1000 ELSE 0 END AS time_seconds FROM results AS T1 INNER JOIN races AS T2 ON T1.raceId = T2.raceId WHERE T1.time IS NOT NULL ), champion_time AS ( SELECT year, raceId, time_seconds FROM time_in_seconds WHERE positionOrder = 1 ) SELECT year, AVG(time_seconds) FROM champion_time WHERE year < 1975 GROUP BY year HAVING AVG(time_seconds) IS NOT NULL
samples: SELECT year, AVG((CAST(SUBSTR(r.time, 1, 2) AS INTEGER) * 3600 + CAST(SUBSTR(r.time, 4, 2) AS INTEGER) * 60 + CAST(SUBSTR(r.time, 7, 2) AS INTEGER)) + (CAST(SUBSTR(r.time, 10) AS INTEGER) / 1000)) AS average_time_seconds FROM results r JOIN driverStandings ds ON r.raceId = ds.raceId WHERE ds.position = 1 AND r.time IS NOT NULL AND r.year < 1975 GROUP BY year;

SELECT year, AVG((CAST(SUBSTR(time, 1, 2) AS INTEGER) * 3600) + (CAST(SUBSTR(time, 4, 2) AS INTEGER) * 60) + CAST(SUBSTR(time, 7, 3) AS INTEGER)) AS average_time FROM results WHERE positionOrder = 1 AND time IS NOT NULL AND year < 1975 GROUP BY year;

SELECT year, AVG((strftime('%s', time) - strftime('%s', '1970-01-01 00:00:00')) + (CAST(SUBSTR(time, 1, 2) AS INTEGER) * 3600) + (CAST(SUBSTR(time, 4, 2) AS INTEGER) * 60) + CAST(SUBSTR(time, 7, 3) AS INTEGER) / 1000) AS avg_time_seconds FROM results JOIN driverStandings ON results.raceId = driverStandings.raceId WHERE driverStandings.position = 1 AND results.time IS NOT NULL AND year < 1975 GROUP BY year;

SELECT year, AVG((CAST(SUBSTR(time, 1, 2) AS INTEGER) * 3600 + CAST(SUBSTR(time, 4, 2) AS INTEGER) * 60 + CAST(SUBSTR(time, 7, 2) AS INTEGER)) + (CAST(SUBSTR(time, 10, 3) AS INTEGER) / 1000)) AS average_time_seconds FROM results WHERE positionOrder = 1 AND time IS NOT NULL AND year < 1975 GROUP BY year;

SELECT year, AVG((strftime('%s', time) - strftime('%s', '1970-01-01')) + (CAST(substr(time, 4, 2) AS INTEGER) * 60) + (CAST(substr(time, 7, 2) AS INTEGER))) AS average_time_seconds FROM results WHERE statusId = (SELECT statusId FROM status WHERE status = 'Finished') AND time IS NOT NULL AND year < 1975 GROUP BY year;

SELECT year, AVG(milliseconds / 1000.0) AS average_time_seconds FROM results INNER JOIN races ON results.raceId = races.raceId WHERE results.positionOrder = 1 AND races.year < 1975 AND results.time IS NOT NULL GROUP BY year;

SELECT year, AVG((CAST(SUBSTR(time, 1, 2) AS INTEGER) * 3600 + CAST(SUBSTR(time, 4, 2) AS INTEGER) * 60 + CAST(SUBSTR(time, 7, 2) AS INTEGER)) + (CAST(SUBSTR(time, 10, 3) AS INTEGER) / 1000)) AS average_time_seconds FROM results WHERE positionOrder = 1 AND time IS NOT NULL AND year < 1975 GROUP BY year;

SELECT year, AVG((CAST(SUBSTR(time, 1, 2) AS INTEGER) * 3600) + (CAST(SUBSTR(time, 4, 2) AS INTEGER) * 60) + CAST(SUBSTR(time, 7, 3) AS INTEGER)) AS avg_time_seconds FROM results WHERE positionOrder = 1 AND time IS NOT NULL AND year < 1975 GROUP BY year;

thrombosis_prediction||1149.question: Are there more in-patient or outpatient who were male? What is the deviation in percentage?

SELECT (SELECT COUNT(ID) FROM Patient WHERE SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '+') * 100.0 / NULLIF((SELECT COUNT(ID) FROM Patient WHERE SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '-'), 0) AS percentage_deviation;
SELECT (COUNT(ID) WHERE SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '+') * 100.0 / COUNT(ID) WHERE SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '-' AS Percentage_Deviation FROM Patient;

SELECT (SELECT COUNT(ID) FROM Patient WHERE SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '+') AS InPatientCount, (SELECT COUNT(ID) FROM Patient WHERE SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '-') AS OutPatientCount, (CAST((SELECT COUNT(ID) FROM Patient WHERE SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '+') AS REAL) / (SELECT COUNT(ID) FROM Patient WHERE SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '-') * 100) AS percentage;

SELECT (COUNT(ID) FILTER (WHERE SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '+') * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(ID) FILTER (WHERE SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '-'), 0)) AS inpatient_percentage, (COUNT(ID) FILTER (WHERE SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '-') * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(ID) FILTER (WHERE SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '+'), 0)) AS outpatient_percentage;

SELECT (SELECT COUNT(ID) FROM Patient WHERE SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '+') AS InPatientCount, (SELECT COUNT(ID) FROM Patient WHERE SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '-') AS OutPatientCount, (SELECT (COUNT(ID) * 1.0 / NULLIF((SELECT COUNT(ID) FROM Patient WHERE SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '-'), 0)) FROM Patient WHERE SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '+') * 100) AS PercentageDeviation;

student_club||1359.question: How many times was the budget in Advertisement for "Yearly Kickoff" meeting more than "October Meeting"?
student_club||1359.evidence: budget in Advertisement refer to category = 'Advertisement' in the budget table; DIVIDE(SUM(amount when event_name = 'Yearly Kickoff'), SUM(amount when event_name = 'October Meeting'))
student_club||1359.gold: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T2.event_name = 'Yearly Kickoff' THEN T1.amount ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / SUM(CASE WHEN T2.event_name = 'October Meeting' THEN T1.amount ELSE 0 END) FROM budget AS T1 INNER JOIN event AS T2 ON T1.link_to_event = T2.event_id WHERE T1.category = 'Advertisement' AND T2.type = 'Meeting'
samples: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM budget WHERE category = 'Advertisement' AND link_to_event IN (SELECT event_id FROM event WHERE event_name = 'Yearly Kickoff') AND (SELECT SUM(amount) FROM budget WHERE category = 'Advertisement' AND link_to_event IN (SELECT event_id FROM event WHERE event_name = 'Yearly Kickoff')) > (SELECT SUM(amount) FROM budget WHERE category = 'Advertisement' AND link_to_event IN (SELECT event_id FROM event WHERE event_name = 'October Meeting'));

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM budget WHERE category = 'Advertisement' AND link_to_event IN (SELECT event_id FROM event WHERE event_name = 'Yearly Kickoff') AND (SELECT SUM(amount) FROM budget WHERE category = 'Advertisement' AND link_to_event IN (SELECT event_id FROM event WHERE event_name = 'Yearly Kickoff')) > (SELECT SUM(amount) FROM budget WHERE category = 'Advertisement' AND link_to_event IN (SELECT event_id FROM event WHERE event_name = 'October Meeting'));

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM budget WHERE category = 'Advertisement' AND link_to_event IN (SELECT event_id FROM event WHERE event_name = 'Yearly Kickoff') AND (SUM(amount) / (SELECT SUM(amount) FROM budget WHERE category = 'Advertisement' AND link_to_event IN (SELECT event_id FROM event WHERE event_name = 'October Meeting'))) > 1;

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM budget WHERE category = 'Advertisement' AND link_to_event IN (SELECT event_id FROM event WHERE event_name = 'Yearly Kickoff') AND (SELECT SUM(amount) FROM budget WHERE category = 'Advertisement' AND link_to_event IN (SELECT event_id FROM event WHERE event_name = 'Yearly Kickoff')) > (SELECT SUM(amount) FROM budget WHERE category = 'Advertisement' AND link_to_event IN (SELECT event_id FROM event WHERE event_name = 'October Meeting'));

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT (SUM(CASE WHEN event_name = 'Yearly Kickoff' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN event_name = 'October Meeting' THEN amount ELSE 0 END), 0)) AS ratio FROM budget WHERE category = 'Advertisement') AS budget_ratio WHERE ratio > 1;

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT (SUM(CASE WHEN event_name = 'Yearly Kickoff' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN event_name = 'October Meeting' THEN amount ELSE 0 END), 0)) AS ratio FROM budget WHERE category = 'Advertisement') AS subquery WHERE ratio > 1;

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM budget WHERE category = 'Advertisement' AND (SELECT SUM(amount) FROM budget WHERE link_to_event IN (SELECT event_id FROM event WHERE event_name = 'Yearly Kickoff')) > (SELECT SUM(amount) FROM budget WHERE link_to_event IN (SELECT event_id FROM event WHERE event_name = 'October Meeting'));

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT (SUM(CASE WHEN event_name = 'Yearly Kickoff' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN event_name = 'October Meeting' THEN amount ELSE 0 END), 0)) > 1 AS is_greater FROM budget WHERE category = 'Advertisement') AS comparison WHERE is_greater = 1;
